title: ranunculus altaicus subsp. taimyrensis
intro: Ranunculus altaicus subsp. taimyrensis, also known as Taimyr crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds, due to its saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
